title: Design an FIR filter from spec to coefficients
description: Translate passband, stopband, and ripple requirements into a tap count and coefficient set using a windowed or equiripple method.
body: Use when a filtering requirement arrives as frequency-domain specifications. Do not pick a tap count by guess, because too few taps misses attenuation and too many wastes cycles. Actions: estimate order from the transition width and attenuation, generate coefficients, then verify the simulated magnitude response against every spec edge. Validation signals include a passband ripple and stopband attenuation that both meet the mask and a group-delay measurement confirming linear phase. Risk boundary: when the order estimate exceeds the cycle budget, escalate to a multirate or cascaded structure rather than silently relaxing the spec.
